memories.scene_title: 5 TWO MEN HIDE IN THE NEARBY WOODS (ZUS & ASAEL BIELSKI) 5
memories.memory_text: I watched the people being herded away and asked where they were taking them, but Zus hushed me—told me to just shut up, please.
grounded_facts: Asael asks 'Where are they taking them?' while observing the deportation.
Zus responds with 'Shveig! Shveig!' and then 'Just shut up! Please...'
Asael speaks again with 'BUT--' before being silenced.

memories.scene_title: 8 A MODEST MILL - LATER THAT DAY 8
memories.memory_text: I saw Papa’s torso behind the trough, just lying there… I couldn’t move, couldn’t speak—only whisper his name into the silence.
grounded_facts: Asael sees his father's torso behind a trough in the mill.
Asael says 'Papa-- Gottenyu...' upon seeing the remains.
The scene includes dead livestock with flies buzzing and a woman's body nearby.

memories.memory_text: I said his name—'Aron'—when I saw him come up from the cellar, but he flinched away, wouldn't let us touch him, wouldn't say a word.
grounded_facts: Asael speaks Aron's name upon seeing him emerge from the root cellar.
Aron recoils when they attempt to embrace him.
Aron does not speak and appears disoriented.

memories.memory_text: I couldn't stop shaking—I was drowning in it, the grief and panic pulling me under, and I couldn't breathe, couldn't think.
grounded_facts: Asael is shaking uncontrollably and crying in the scene.
The script describes Asael as 'lost in a confusion of grief and panic.'

memories.scene_title: 12 ASAEL AND ARON SLEEP HUDDLED ON THE GROUND 12
memories.memory_text: I heard a footstep in the dark and then saw him—Tuvia. I jumped up and ran to him, holding on as if I’d drown if I let go.
grounded_facts: Asael hears a footstep and sits up in fear.
Asael recognizes Tuvia and immediately embraces him.
The embrace is described as desperate, like Tuvia is salvation.

memories.scene_title: 12 ASAEL AND ARON SLEEP HUDDLED ON THE GROUND 12
memories.memory_text: I told him—my voice shaking—'Papa and Mama. They killed them.' I couldn’t let go, not even after I said it.
grounded_facts: Asael speaks the line 'Papa and Mama. They killed them.'
Asael continues to cling to Tuvia after speaking.
The revelation is delivered emotionally and immediately after the embrace.

memories.memory_text: I stood outside the farmhouse, heart pounding, scanning the trees and road for any movement while Zus was inside—every second felt like a breath held too long.
grounded_facts: Asael waited outside the farmhouse while Zus went inside to get food.
Asael was on guard and felt anxious during this time.

memories.memory_text: I can't believe Papa's dead. The words came out before I could stop them, right after we laughed like we used to—like nothing had changed.
grounded_facts: Asael says 'I can't believe Papa's dead' in the scene
The statement follows a moment of shared laughter with his brothers imitating their father
The emotional shift from laughter to grief is immediate and personal

memories.memory_text: Koscik has a pistol. I remembered that suddenly—how he once showed it to me—and I said it out loud, like it might be the first real thing we could do instead of just running.
grounded_facts: Asael states 'Koscik has a pistol. He showed it to me once.'
This line introduces the idea of armed resistance in the scene
The memory reflects Asael's recollection of a past interaction with Koscik

memories.memory_text: I tried to keep the fire going, but the kindling was wet and wouldn't catch—then Shimon tore pages from a Hebrew book and lit them, and the flames finally rose.
grounded_facts: Asael was attempting to start or maintain a fire for cooking.
The kindling was wet, making it difficult to ignite.
Shimon contributed by ripping pages from a book and using them to start the fire.

memories.memory_text: Tuvia called my name sharply—'Asael!'—and told me to find food for the new people, his voice leaving no room for hesitation, so I moved toward the stores, already calculating how thin we could stretch the soup.
grounded_facts: Tuvia directly addressed Asael and instructed him to find food for the new arrivals.
The instruction occurred in front of others, in a moment of group tension about resources.
Asael would need to access or manage food supplies to fulfill the order.

memories.memory_text: I brushed Aron's hair while the others gathered around the food, trying to keep some sense of care in the midst of all this loss.
grounded_facts: Asael brushed Aron's hair during the food distribution at Camp #1.
This occurred while new arrivals were present and others were nearby, including Rachel, Tuvia, Malbin, and Zus.
The act is described directly in the scene text with no added interpretation.

memories.memory_text: I asked what an 'otriad' meant, and when Tuvia said it was an armed brigade, I said it right then—'Then we are the Bielski Otriad.'
grounded_facts: Asael asked Tuvia for the meaning of the word 'otriad'.
Tuvia defined 'otriad' as an armed brigade.
Asael declared the group to be the 'Bielski Otriad' immediately after learning the definition.

memories.scene_title: 44 A ROADSIDE DITCH - LATER THAT NIGHT 44
memories.memory_text: I lay beside Tuvia in the ditch, cold earth under me, watching him loop the wire around the post—one end already set, taut across the road, waiting.
grounded_facts: Asael is lying beside Tuvia on one side of a road.
Tuvia is attaching one end of a wire to a post.
A wire is stretched across the road as part of an ambush setup.

memories.memory_text: I saw the German soldier spot us and turn to run—I didn’t think, I just moved with Peretz, feet pounding the road, Jacov behind us with the rifle ready.
grounded_facts: Asael is seen by the German private along with Peretz.
Asael participates in the chase immediately after the private turns and runs.
Jacov follows Asael and Peretz during the pursuit, armed with a rifle.

memories.memory_text: I remember closing in on the soldier, the crunch of gravel under my boots as Peretz and I brought him down, kicking until Jacov reached us and fired the final shot.
grounded_facts: Asael catches up to the soldier with Peretz after Jacov wounds him.
Asael participates in knocking the soldier to the ground and kicking him.
Asael is present when Jacov fires the final shot.

memories.memory_text: I didn't know where to go—I just ran, crashing into the wheat field, but it was the wrong side of the road. The spotlight hit me, and I saw soldiers pouring out, coming straight for me.
grounded_facts: Asael panicked and fled into the wheat field on the wrong side of the road.
A truck used its spotlight to reveal Asael.
Fifteen soldiers began pursuing Asael after he was exposed.

memories.memory_text: I realized too late that I'd gone too far—I had to double back toward the woods, but the soldiers were already moving to cut me off.
grounded_facts: Asael realizes he has made a tactical error.
Asael attempts to circle back toward the woods.
Soldiers move to cut Asael off before he reaches the woods.
Asael does not reach the woods and is intercepted.

memories.memory_text: I dove to my knees and crawled as fast as I could, digging into the mud of the ditch with my hands, feeling it tear under my nails while voices closed in around me.
grounded_facts: Asael dives to his knees and disappears from view after the spotlight shatters.
Asael crawls to avoid exposure and reaches an irrigation ditch.
Asael frantically digs into the soft mud on the ditch's banks, scraping his hands raw.
Asael hears pursuers nearby calling out to each other.

memories.memory_text: I stepped out of the dark cellar into the blinding light, and suddenly Zus was hugging me so tight I couldn’t breathe—I barely had time to register the sun before his arms crushed me.
grounded_facts: Asael emerges from the root cellar into bright light
Zus pulls Asael into a tight embrace
Asael says 'Zus.... ZUSSIE... I can't breathe'

memories.memory_text: I reached back into the cellar and brought Bella and Chaya out, their dresses dirty but fine, and when I showed the rifles and ammunition, I saw the way the others looked at me—not just as a survivor, but as someone who brought hope.
grounded_facts: Asael reaches back into the root cellar
Bella and Chaya emerge after Asael
Asael presents two rifles and a large box of ammunition
Zus eyes the women appreciatively
Tuvia is present and observes the supplies

memories.memory_text: I grabbed Chaya's hand and shouted 'Don't panic. THIS WAY! GO!' as gunfire broke out, pulling her and the others toward the tree line while Tuvia yelled at me to keep moving.
grounded_facts: Asael is instructed by Tuvia to keep the group moving during the evacuation.
Asael physically leads people, including Chaya, during the escape.
Asael speaks the line 'Don't panic. THIS WAY! GO!' in the scene.
Gunfire occurs during the evacuation, with two group members killed.

memories.scene_title: 93 MEANWHILE, THE WOMEN WATCH AS ASAEL LEADS IN NEW ARRIVALS 93
memories.memory_text: I put my arm around Chaya as she started to cry—her body shaking against me—and then she looked up, her eyes searching mine like she was trying to find some promise there.
grounded_facts: Asael physically comforted Chaya by putting his arm around her when she began crying.
Chaya was visibly emotional and crying after hearing about the liquidation of the ghetto and the danger to her parents.

memories.scene_title: 93 MEANWHILE, THE WOMEN WATCH AS ASAEL LEADS IN NEW ARRIVALS 93
memories.memory_text: She held my gaze and asked if I would help her parents, then said if I rescued them, she would do anything for me—I froze, my voice caught, and I stammered out a confused yes.
grounded_facts: Chaya looked deeply into Asael's eyes and directly asked him to help rescue her parents.
Chaya implied a conditional offer—'I would do anything'—in exchange for the rescue.
Asael responded with hesitation, stammering 'I...I...', then gave a confused and conflicted agreement: 'No. I mean, yes. All right.'

memories.memory_text: I remember how exposed I felt stepping beyond the tree line—just open ground between us and the city, no cover, nothing. The air was colder out there, and I could see Novogrudok rising in the distance like a warning.
grounded_facts: Asael left the forest with Tuvia and felt vulnerable doing so.
The city of Novogrudok was visible in the distance from the outskirts.

memories.memory_text: Zus pulled me into an embrace and whispered 'Hide this' as he shoved a revolver into my pocket—my hands trembled as I realized I was being trusted with something dangerous, something secret.
grounded_facts: Zus whispered 'Hide this' to Asael during an embrace.
Zus placed a revolver into Asael's pocket.
Zus informed Asael that Wolnicky stores ammunition in his barn and that there is no guard.

memories.scene_title: 150 IN REAL TIME -- THE CAMP IS BEING TORN APART 150
memories.memory_text: I shouted at Malbin to get them out of the bunkers — he hesitated, then ran. I couldn’t tell if he understood, but he moved.
grounded_facts: Asael yells at Malbin to evacuate the people from the bunkers.
Malbin runs off after being yelled at.

memories.scene_title: 150 IN REAL TIME -- THE CAMP IS BEING TORN APART 150
memories.memory_text: Tuvia couldn’t hear, so I mimed firing a rifle and pointed to positions — he nodded, but I wasn’t sure if he truly understood until he finally agreed.
grounded_facts: Asael uses pantomime to communicate holding off the enemy.
Asael assigns defensive positions by pointing.
Tuvia initially shakes his head but then nods in agreement.

memories.scene_title: 153 MEANWHILE -- THE ASSIGNED DEFENDERS, MEN AND WOMEN ALIKE 153
memories.memory_text: Tuvia grabbed my shoulders and told me not to fight—just to slow them down and then join the group. I nodded because I understood: this was the last order I might ever follow.
grounded_facts: Tuvia physically grabbed Asael's shoulders.
Tuvia gave Asael the instruction: 'Don't try to fight... Just slow them down. Then join us.'
Asael responded with a nod, indicating understanding and acceptance.

memories.scene_title: 153 MEANWHILE -- THE ASSIGNED DEFENDERS, MEN AND WOMEN ALIKE 153
memories.memory_text: I pushed Chaya toward Tuvia and told her to go—she clung to my arm for a second, but I made her leave. I don’t know if I’ll see her again.
grounded_facts: Asael pushed Chaya toward Tuvia.
Asael said 'Take her.'
Chaya resisted momentarily by clinging to Asael's arm.

memories.memory_text: I shook my head when Rifka suggested I take false papers and stay in the city — I couldn't betray who I was, not while my family was out there.
grounded_facts: Tuvia refused Rifka's suggestion to stay in the city and pass as a gentile.
Tuvia expressed his need to go to his family.
Tuvia physically shook his head in response to Rifka's proposal.

memories.scene_title: 12 ASAEL AND ARON SLEEP HUDDLED ON THE GROUND 12
memories.memory_text: I heard the footstep, then Asael leapt into my arms like I was the only thing keeping him alive — I held both him and Aron, silent, feeling the weight of what I had to carry now.
grounded_facts: Asael immediately embraced Tuvia upon recognizing him.
Aron allowed himself to be held by Tuvia.
Tuvia held his younger brothers silently after learning of their parents' death.

memories.scene_title: 12 ASAEL AND ARON SLEEP HUDDLED ON THE GROUND 12
memories.memory_text: Zus appeared with a hatchet, looked at me like he wasn’t sure who I was anymore, then tossed me the mezuzah — that one gesture, 'Welcome home,' felt like both a test and a passing of responsibility.
grounded_facts: Zus appeared holding a hatchet.
Tuvia and Zus exchanged a wary look.
Zus took the mezuzah from his pocket and tossed it to Tuvia.
Zus said 'Welcome home' as he gave Tuvia the mezuzah.

memories.memory_text: I remember standing in the barn, Koscik pressing me to take in the laborers, and I could only ask—what am I supposed to do with them? How do I feed more when I can't even care for my own?
grounded_facts: Tuvia is in the barn speaking with Koscik in hushed, tense tones.
Koscik warns that if the laborers are found, he will be denounced.
Tuvia expresses doubt about taking in more refugees, citing inability to feed them and care for his own family.
Refugees are present and silently watching Tuvia with hostile expressions.

memories.memory_text: I stood in the doorway, rain soaking through my clothes, and asked the police captain if he knew who I was—he said nothing, but his wife’s face told me she understood the reckoning had come.
grounded_facts: Tuvia enters the house during heavy rain, standing at the threshold with a pistol.
He speaks the line 'You know who I am?' to the Police Captain.
The Police Captain remains silent; his wife shows immediate fear.

memories.memory_text: After I shot the first son lunging at me, the mother screamed and the captain tried to rise—I backed away and fired again, then turned the gun on the second son; it was over in seconds, but their faces are still with me.
grounded_facts: One son stands and moves toward Tuvia, prompting a fatal shot to the chest.
The mother screams after her son is shot.
The Police Captain begins to stand and is shot, followed by the second son.
The entire sequence of shootings occurs rapidly, within seconds.

memories.memory_text: I told Zus they are family — I had to say it firmly, even as he pulled me aside, because if we don’t hold that line, we lose what makes us more than just survivors.
grounded_facts: Tuvia says 'They are family' in response to Zus's concern.
Zus takes Tuvia aside to question the decision to accept more refugees.
Tuvia asserts the group's unity despite logistical concerns.

memories.memory_text: I called for Asael to find food — not just for one, but for all of them — and I meant it, even if it means thinner soup for everyone.
grounded_facts: Tuvia says 'Asael, find them something to eat.'
Tuvia later says 'We'll water the soup' to address concerns about resources.
The newcomers are visibly in need, arriving tired and carrying little.

memories.scene_title: 42 MOMENTS LATER - JUST OUTSIDE OF CAMP -- 42
memories.memory_text: I told Zus we can't become like them, that taking lives won't bring his family back, and for a moment I saw the same ghosts in his eyes that I carry.
grounded_facts: Tuvia said 'We cannot become...like them.'
Tuvia referenced Zus's dead family, saying 'It won't bring your family back to life. Or Mama. Or Papa.'
Tuvia expressed shared trauma by saying 'And I can still see their faces.'

memories.scene_title: 42 MOMENTS LATER - JUST OUTSIDE OF CAMP -- 42
memories.memory_text: When he named Asael, Peretz, and Jacov as his men, I felt the ground shift—then I asked him, 'You are making the decisions now?' and the silence after hung like a blade.
grounded_facts: Zus listed Peretz, Jacov, and Asael as part of his group.
Asael avoided eye contact when named.
Tuvia responded with 'You are making the decisions now?'
A stand-off occurred between Tuvia and Zus, indicating a power struggle.

memories.memory_text: I tackled the Lieutenant as he reached for his pistol, and we rolled across the road fighting—clawing, punching, biting—until I slammed his head against the pavement.
grounded_facts: Tuvia tackled the Lieutenant when he tried to draw a pistol.
The gun skidded away during the struggle.
Tuvia and the Lieutenant fought on the ground using close physical combat.
Tuvia slammed the Lieutenant's head against the pavement.

memories.memory_text: The gunfire could be heard for miles—I said it out loud, my voice tight with worry, because the sound might bring more of them down on us.
grounded_facts: Tuvia expresses concern that the gunfire could be heard for miles
This concern is voiced immediately after the shooting and before receiving the weapon

memories.memory_text: I took the Schmeisser from Zus and checked it fast—chamber, grip, weight—making sure it was ready, because now there was no turning back.
grounded_facts: Tuvia accepts the Schmeisser from Zus
He checks the gun efficiently
indicating readiness and familiarity

memories.memory_text: Shimon stared at me, saying they thought I was bringing food, and kept pressing—did the farmers have guns, did they have dogs? I couldn’t answer. I just looked at him, while all around me, hungry faces watched, waiting for something I couldn’t give.
grounded_facts: Shimon confronted Tuvia about not bringing food.
Shimon questioned whether farmers had stopped Tuvia with guns or dogs.
Tuvia did not respond to Shimon's questions.
Other members of the camp were visibly hungry and watching the exchange.

memories.memory_text: Zus was drunk, shouting at Lazar, slapping him over and over—I had to pull him off. When he lunged again, I yanked him back hard, sent him falling. I told him no mistakes, then reminded him of what would happen to Asael if we weren’t careful.
grounded_facts: Zus was intoxicated and attacked Lazar physically.
Tuvia intervened by pulling Zus off Lazar.
Tuvia physically subdued Zus, causing him to fall.
Tuvia referenced the danger to Asael as a warning about consequences of recklessness.

memories.memory_text: I saw Asael emerge from the root cellar, blinking in the light, and I said, 'Thank God.'
grounded_facts: Asael came out of the root cellar after being hidden.
The light was bright when he emerged.
Tuvia spoke the words 'Thank God' upon seeing him.

memories.memory_text: I turned to Koscik's wife and said, 'Thank you,' before she turned away and told us to go.
grounded_facts: Koscik's wife opened the door to the root cellar and helped in the rescue.
Tuvia expressed gratitude by saying 'Thank you' to her.
She did not acknowledge the thanks and immediately told them to leave.

memories.scene_title: 73 WALKING THROUGH THE CAMP TUVIA AND ASAEL 73
memories.memory_text: I told Asael to go talk to Chaya after I caught him staring at her—she turned when I called her name, and he blushed like a boy.
grounded_facts: Tuvia noticed Asael was distracted by Chaya.
Tuvia called out Chaya's name to draw her attention.
Asael appeared embarrassed when Chaya looked over.

memories.scene_title: 73 WALKING THROUGH THE CAMP TUVIA AND ASAEL 73
memories.memory_text: I told Asael to go with Zus to the Piznarksi granary to make sure he understood my orders.
grounded_facts: Tuvia instructed Asael to accompany Zus on a mission.
The mission included checking the Piznarksi granary.
Tuvia wanted Asael to ensure Zus understood his orders.

memories.memory_text: I remember sitting there, the violin playing outside, and hearing Shimon offer to say Kaddish—but I barely heard him. I was thinking only of her: how she called me a failure, how we weren't really married, how I couldn't give her what she needed.
grounded_facts: Tuvia is sitting alone under a shelter while the sound of a violin plays outside.
Shimon approaches and offers condolences and the suggestion to say Kaddish.
Tuvia does not respond directly to Shimon but speaks introspectively about his wife.
Tuvia reflects on his wife's coldness and his perceived failure as a provider.
His speech is described as being almost to himself, indicating internal reflection.

memories.memory_text: I told Zus I was going in with him, even though he ordered me to stay with the truck. He said he gave the orders tonight, but I couldn't stay behind.
grounded_facts: Tuvia says 'I'm going in with you' after Zus tells him to stay with the truck.
Zus asserts authority by saying 'I give the orders tonight.'
Tuvia defies the order implicitly by not complying and preparing to join.

memories.memory_text: I gave Zus our parents' mezuzah before he left the truck. I don't know if it was for his luck or mine, but he took it without a word and walked into the dark.
grounded_facts: Tuvia takes out the mezuzah from his pocket that once blessed their parents' home.
He hands it to Zus saying, 'For luck.'
Zus pockets it and leaves without verbal response.

memories.memory_text: I felt the weight of Zus’s words like a blow when he said fighters die so the malbushim can live — the bitterness in his voice, the blood on the package, it all hit me at once.
grounded_facts: Zus made an accusation that fighters die so that malbushim may live.
Tuvia absorbed the accusation physically and emotionally, as if taking a punch.
The medicine was wrapped in a blood-soaked package.

memories.memory_text: I asked him to come back to us, and when I said 'You are my brother,' he only replied, 'So you remember,' without even looking at me.
grounded_facts: Tuvia said 'Come back to us' to Zus.
Tuvia said 'You are my brother' in a personal appeal.
Zus did not look at Tuvia and responded with 'So you remember.'

memories.scene_title: 121 IN A ZEMYLANKA - LATER THAT DAY 121
memories.memory_text: I can't stop coughing, the fever won't break, and the bottle is empty again—but I can still hear them arguing outside, as if I'm not falling apart in here.
grounded_facts: Tuvia is drinking heavily.
Tuvia is coughing steadily.
Tuvia has a fever and is sweating.
Angry voices are heard from outside the bunker.

memories.memory_text: I told both Arkady and Asael their rations would be cut for a week—same punishment for both—even though Asael looked at me like I’d betrayed him, and the others were watching to see if I’d back down.
grounded_facts: Tuvia declares reduced rations for one week for both Arkady and Asael.
Asael is astonished and embarrassed by Tuvia's decision and public drunkenness.
Others are closely watching the confrontation as a test of Tuvia's authority.

memories.memory_text: When Asael told me the rumors—that I drink too much and can’t lead—part of me wanted to deny it, but all I could say was 'Maybe they're right,' and then I walked away before he could see more.
grounded_facts: Asael reveals rumors that Tuvia is power-hungry, corrupt, takes women, hoards gold, and drinks too much to lead.
Tuvia responds with 'Maybe they're right,' showing doubt.
Tuvia reels away toward the woods after the exchange.

memories.memory_text: I said it plainly, weak and burning with fever—'I am not a leader'—because I couldn't carry it anymore, not even in the dark of the bunker.
grounded_facts: Tuvia is in his bunker, sick with fever and vomiting.
Shimon brings him soup and makes a comment about leadership and Moses.
Tuvia responds with 'I am not a leader,' rejecting the role during a moment of physical and emotional collapse.

memories.memory_text: I told her I couldn't keep the living alive — that Tamara and the father were responsible — but then she said the words that stopped me: 'She was raped by the Nazis.' I closed my eyes. I was so tired of deciding who lived and who didn't.
grounded_facts: Tuvia states he cannot keep the living alive and assigns responsibility to Tamara and the child's father.
Lilka reveals the baby was conceived through rape by Nazis, which visibly affects Tuvia.
Tuvia closes his eyes and feels emotional exhaustion after hearing the revelation.

memories.memory_text: After she kissed me — first softly, then deeper — I remember the smell of her washed hair, the warmth of her skin. I whispered her name, and when she said she wasn't afraid of me, something broke inside. I didn't stop the kiss.
grounded_facts: Lilka initiates a chaste kiss, followed by a deeper, passionate kiss.
Tuvia perceives sensory details: the smell of Lilka's hair and the glow of her skin.
Lilka says she was never afraid of Tuvia, and they continue kissing.

memories.scene_title: 138 TUVIA STANDS ALONE IN THE FOREST, HIS FACE IN SHADOW 138
memories.memory_text: I knelt beside Aron, holding him as he wept silently under the tree, and whispered to the Lord, begging for mercy after all we’ve done.
grounded_facts: Tuvia saw Aron crying beside a tree
Tuvia knelt and cradled Aron
Tuvia prayed to the Lord of the Universe, referencing unspeakable acts and seeking mercy

memories.scene_title: 138 TUVIA STANDS ALONE IN THE FOREST, HIS FACE IN SHADOW 138
memories.memory_text: I heard Malbin’s voice from the edge of the forest—pale, shaken—and the words 'Viktor is here' cut through the silence, stopping my thoughts.
grounded_facts: Tuvia heard Malbin arrive at the forest's edge
Malbin appeared drawn and pale
Malbin announced 'Viktor is here'

memories.memory_text: Viktor demanded I hand over command of my fighters, saying his orders gave him authority over all partisans—and that if I refused, I'd be labeled a deserter.
grounded_facts: Viktor stated he had orders to take command of all partisan operations. Viktor told Tuvia that refusing would make him a deserter. Tuvia asked what would happen if he refused. The conversation occurred after Viktor examined documents from a prisoner.

memories.memory_text: He handed me a German wanted poster with my face on it—fifty thousand marks for my capture—and said the likeness was too Jewish, as if it were a joke.
grounded_facts: Viktor produced a German wanted flyer offering fifty thousand marks for Tuvia's capture. Viktor commented that the image on the poster looked too Jewish. Viktor physically handed the flyer to Tuvia. Asael later took the poster from Tuvia.

memories.scene_title: 144 THE SCREAMING WHISTLE OF THE FIRST BOMBER 144
memories.memory_text: I grabbed people and dragged them into cover as the cannon fire tore up the ground—I had to move fast before more were hit.
grounded_facts: Tuvia physically intervened by grabbing and dragging people to cover during the bombing.
Strafing fire from a 20mm cannon struck the ground, endangering the group.
Some individuals were hit before reaching safety.

memories.scene_title: 153 MEANWHILE -- THE ASSIGNED DEFENDERS, MEN AND WOMEN ALIKE 153
memories.memory_text: I grabbed Asael by the shoulders and told him not to fight, just slow them down and fall back — we couldn’t afford a stand, only survival.
grounded_facts: Tuvia physically grabbed Asael's shoulders.
Tuvia gave Asael the order to avoid fighting and instead delay the enemy before retreating.
Asael nodded in understanding, indicating silent agreement.

memories.scene_title: 153 MEANWHILE -- THE ASSIGNED DEFENDERS, MEN AND WOMEN ALIKE 153
memories.memory_text: Chaya resisted when I tried to take her — she was clinging to Asael — but I forced her to come with me, pulling her away to keep moving.
grounded_facts: Asael pushed Chaya toward Tuvia.
Chaya resisted, clinging to Asael's arm momentarily.
Tuvia compelled Chaya to leave with him despite her resistance.

memories.scene_title: 162 A RISE REVEALS THE IMMENSITY OF THE KRASNAYA GORKA SWAMP 162
memories.memory_text: I dropped to my knees, staring at the swamp—Asael wasn't coming back, and I had no answer, no order to give. The weight of all their eyes on me, but I couldn't speak.
grounded_facts: Tuvia sinks to his knees and is unable to speak when he believes Asael is dead.
He is silent and unresponsive as others look to him for direction.
He stares at the swamp, which is described as impenetrable.

memories.scene_title: 162 A RISE REVEALS THE IMMENSITY OF THE KRASNAYA GORKA SWAMP 162
memories.memory_text: Lilka knelt beside me and took my hand—her touch pulled me back, even as the plane appeared above. I felt the moment shift, something reigniting beneath the numbness.
grounded_facts: Lilka kneels beside Tuvia and takes his hand silently.
A spotter plane appears overhead at the same time.
Tuvia begins to emerge from his silence and paralysis after Lilka's gesture.

memories.memory_text: I couldn't help it—we were all laughing, lying there on the ground, soaked and shaking, but finally safe. The sound spread through us like fire, and for a moment, none of the weight was on my shoulders.
grounded_facts: Tuvia laughed with the group after reaching the opposite bank
The laughter was spontaneous and spread through the group
Tuvia was physically exhausted and lying on the ground

memories.memory_text: I knelt beside Shimon as he whispered that I was sent by God to save them. I called it ridiculous, but when he thanked me—and Him—I couldn't hold back the tears.
grounded_facts: Tuvia knelt beside Shimon at his request
Shimon whispered that Tuvia was sent by God to save the group
Tuvia responded with 'Ridiculous' but was visibly moved
Tuvia cried despite trying to control his emotions

memories.memory_text: I stepped toward the farmer with the pitchfork, ready to make him give us food, but Asael held me back—I had to say it, that we’d be back, so he’d know this wasn’t over.
grounded_facts: Zus approaches the farmer menacingly after being threatened with a pitchfork.
Asael restrains Zus and urges him to leave.
Zus delivers the line 'We'll be back' while retreating toward the woods.

memories.memory_text: I heard a noise from the cupboard and grabbed the hatchet—someone could be inside, could be a threat, and I won’t let anything happen to us.
grounded_facts: Zus hears a noise from a cupboard.
Zus grabs a hatchet in response.

memories.memory_text: When the boy came up from the root cellar—Aron—I moved to embrace him, but he flinched back, silent and scared, and I had to stop myself from reaching further.
grounded_facts: A young boy (Aron) emerges from the root cellar.
Zus attempts to embrace Aron.
Aron recoils and will not speak.

memories.memory_text: I grabbed Asael, shouting at him to stop—his crying, his shaking—it was too much, and I didn’t know what else to do.
grounded_facts: Zus shouts 'Stop it...' multiple times in escalating intensity.
Asael is crying and shaking uncontrollably.
Zus physically grabs and holds Asael in a gesture that mixes violence and comfort.

memories.scene_title: 12 ASAEL AND ARON SLEEP HUDDLED ON THE GROUND 12
memories.memory_text: I saw the mezuzah in my hand, then tossed it to Tuvia—no words needed. He caught it, and that was that.
grounded_facts: Zus had the mezuzah in his pocket.
Zus took it out and tossed it to Tuvia.
The act occurred after Tuvia returned and confirmed he had seen their parents.

memories.memory_text: I raised my rifle and thought about crushing that man's head — he's lucky I didn't, after all the shit I've eaten from his kind.
grounded_facts: Zus raised his rifle in triumph while running through the woods.
Zus stated, 'He's lucky I didn't crush his head.'
Zus referred to a man as a 'Fucking Polack' and expressed long-standing resentment toward Poles.

memories.memory_text: When Tuvia questioned me, I told him plainly — no, not all, but some I will kill, because this ends now.
grounded_facts: Tuvia challenged Zus about terrorizing anti-Semites.
Zus responded, 'No. Some I will kill,' indicating a selective but deliberate intent to enact violence.

memories.memory_text: I laughed when Tuvia smacked me and mocked me like Papa used to—right after I farted. Even with Papa gone, that old stupid joke still hit.
grounded_facts: Zus farted in the clearing.
Tuvia smacked Zus on the head.
Tuvia imitated their father's voice, calling Zus a peasant.
Asael and Zus both laughed despite their grief.

memories.memory_text: I told Tuvia we can't feed these people, and then made sure he remembered to ask Koscik for the pistol—after he asked for food.
grounded_facts: Zus expressed concern that they could not feed the survivors.
Zus reminded Tuvia to retrieve the pistol from Koscik.
Tuvia agreed to ask for the pistol after initially agreeing to ask for food.
Zus spoke to Tuvia out of earshot of others.

memories.memory_text: I sent Tuvia to get food, and instead he brought back more hungry mouths—no supplies, just another burden. When I asked if he got the gun, he showed me only four bullets.
grounded_facts: Zus questioned Tuvia about retrieving the gun after a mission.
Tuvia returned with only four bullets.
Zus expressed frustration that Tuvia brought new people instead of securing resources.

memories.scene_title: 38 NEXT MORNING - A LOW FOG CLINGS TO THE FOREST FLOOR 38
memories.memory_text: I took the pistol from Tuvia’s belt and checked the cylinder—only one bullet left. I asked him how it felt to kill them, and he told me to shut up.
grounded_facts: Zus took the pistol from Tuvia's belt.
Zus checked the cylinder and observed only one bullet remaining.
Zus asked Tuvia about killing the sons, and Tuvia responded with 'Shut up.'

memories.scene_title: 38 NEXT MORNING - A LOW FOG CLINGS TO THE FOREST FLOOR 38
memories.memory_text: I looked at the huddled people—women, children, the old teacher—and said they’d slow us down. Tuvia joked, but I meant it: we can’t move fast with them.
grounded_facts: Zus observed the group sleeping huddled together, including women and children.
Zus questioned the group's ability to survive due to the presence of non-combatants.
Zus specifically mentioned the women and Tuvia's old teacher as liabilities.
Tuvia responded with sarcasm about the teacher punishing Zus for homework.

memories.scene_title: 42 MOMENTS LATER - JUST OUTSIDE OF CAMP -- 42
memories.memory_text: I loaded Wilencki's gun while Tuvia kept talking about not making enemies, but all I could think was blood for blood — his mercy won't bring back Mama or Papa.
grounded_facts: Zus was loading Wilencki's gun during the conversation with Tuvia.
Tuvia argued against killing, emphasizing restraint and the futility of revenge.
Zus responded with 'Blood for blood' and referenced Tuvia having already taken revenge.

memories.scene_title: 42 MOMENTS LATER - JUST OUTSIDE OF CAMP -- 42
memories.memory_text: I asked Asael to come with us — Peretz, Jacov, and me — and when Tuvia challenged me, I looked him in the eye and made him ask, 'You are making the decisions now?'
grounded_facts: Zus explicitly included Asael, Peretz, and Jacov in his plan to leave.
Tuvia questioned Zus's authority by asking, 'You are making the decisions now?'
Asael avoided eye contact when named, indicating tension in the moment.

memories.scene_title: 43A A CLUSTER OF HOUSES - LATER THAT NIGHT 43A
memories.memory_text: Peretz and I were backing out of that house, shooting into the dark as screams rose behind us — I could feel the kick of my rifle, the heat of it, and the weight of what we’d just done.
grounded_facts: Zus was firing from inside a doorway alongside Peretz
There were screams coming from inside the house during the shooting
The action involved retreating while shooting backward

memories.scene_title: 43A A CLUSTER OF HOUSES - LATER THAT NIGHT 43A
memories.memory_text: I shouted into the night — 'We are the Bielski Otriad! We are Jews!' — faces watched from windows, silent, and I made them see us, made them know we weren’t running anymore.
grounded_facts: Zus declared 'We are the Bielski Otriad! We are Jews!' aloud to the neighborhood
Residents' faces appeared at windows in response
The proclamation was delivered immediately after the raid

memories.scene_title: 45 IN THE DITCH ON THE OTHER SIDE 45
memories.memory_text: I wrapped the wire tight around the tree with Peretz and Jacov, making sure it was secure for the ambush.
grounded_facts: Zus participated in wrapping wire to a tree alongside Peretz and Jacov.
The action was part of setting up ambush infrastructure.

memories.memory_text: I found a beautiful knife in the saddlebags after we took the motorcyclist down—lifted it up like a trophy, felt the weight of it in my hand.
grounded_facts: Zus rummaged through the saddlebags of the downed motorcycle.
Zus found a knife.
Zus lifted the knife up triumphantly.

memories.memory_text: I smashed the Sergeant's nose with the knife handle and ripped the Schmeisser from him, firing point-blank—his coat caught fire as he went down.
grounded_facts: Zus used the handle of a knife to break the Sergeant's nose
Zus took the Schmeisser submachine gun from the Sergeant
Zus fired the Schmeisser at close range, killing the Sergeant
The Sergeant's greatcoat caught fire during the attack

memories.memory_text: I swung the Schmeisser toward the staff car and opened fire, shattering the windows and dropping the officer and the woman inside—Tuvia was clear, alive.
grounded_facts: Zus turned the Schmeisser toward the staff car
Zus fired the weapon into the car
The windows of the car were blown out by gunfire
The officer and the woman inside were killed
Tuvia was saved as a result of the intervention

memories.memory_text: I was high from the kill, blood all over me, and I didn't care about the noise—Tuvia could say whatever he wanted, but that man pissed on my head and now he's dead.
grounded_facts: Zus killed a man and is covered in his blood
Zus feels euphoric and energized after the killing
Zus reacts to Tuvia's criticism with defiance, justifying the act by referencing the insult

memories.memory_text: I handed Tuvia the Schmeisser like a prize—he took it without a word, and I kept moving, telling Peretz and Jacov to search the car and me to check the body for ammo.
grounded_facts: Zus gives the Schmeisser to Tuvia as if presenting a trophy
Zus issues orders to Peretz and Jacov to search the car
Zus intends to search the dead man's pockets for more ammunition

memories.memory_text: I ran back under fire to grab Peretz’s rifle after we got him into the trees—he bled in my hands and I wasn’t leaving his weapon for the enemy.
grounded_facts: Peretz was shot while escaping with Tuvia and Zus.
Zus helped carry Peretz into the forest.
Zus returned to retrieve Peretz's rifle after reaching safety.

memories.memory_text: Tuvia ripped my rifle out of my hands—just as I was about to act—and shot the spotlight, throwing everything into blackness.
grounded_facts: Tuvia and Zus were both present and observing the scene with growing horror.
Tuvia took the rifle from Zus by force.
Tuvia used the rifle to shoot and shatter the spotlight, causing darkness.

memories.memory_text: I was crouched in the woods with Tuvia and Ben Zion, waiting in silence until Tuvia said 'Now'—then we moved out, weapons up, closing in on the farm.
grounded_facts: Zus was present in the woods with Tuvia and Ben Zion.
They were observing Kosciak's farm in silence.
Tuvia gave the order to advance.
Zus left cover and approached the farm with his weapon ready.

memories.memory_text: I called out to Koscik, told him to stop fooling with the sheep and pull up his pants—then I saw him hanging from the threshing hook, that sign swinging from his chest like some sick joke.
grounded_facts: Zus called out to Koscik with a mocking tone before discovering the body.
Zus saw Koscik's corpse hanging from a threshing hook driven through his chest.
A sign reading 'JEW LOVER' in Polish was attached to the hook.

memories.memory_text: I pulled Asael into a mighty embrace — my brother, alive and standing in the light after all this time. I couldn’t speak, just held him tight until he joked I was crushing him.
grounded_facts: Zus embraced Asael upon his emergence from the root cellar.
Asael spoke to Zus during the embrace, saying 'Zus.... ZUSSIE... I can't breathe.'
The reunion occurred after a prolonged separation, implied by the emotional weight of the moment.

memories.memory_text: When Bella stepped out behind Asael, I looked her over — dirty dress, but it was clear she was something special. I said, 'Asael... Well, well..' and meant every bit of it.
grounded_facts: Bella emerged from the root cellar after Asael.
Zus observed Bella and Chaya as they came out.
Zus made a remark — 'Asael... Well, well..' — immediately after seeing Bella, indicating interest.
Bella’s clothing was dirty but once expensive, a detail visible upon her emergence.

memories.memory_text: I was still breathing hard from being with Bella in the woods—her skin warm against mine—when I heard the screaming. I didn’t have time to think, just grabbed my rifle and snapped, 'QUIET! NOT ANOTHER WORD!!!' like a command from my bones.
grounded_facts: Zus was with Bella in the woods immediately before the attack.
Zus grabs his rifle as the camp is attacked.
Zus shouts 'QUIET! NOT ANOTHER WORD!!!' during the chaos.

memories.memory_text: The gunfire cracked through the camp, and I saw two bodies drop—no time to process, only to act. I barked the order to silence everyone, knowing one more sound could bring the police straight to us.
grounded_facts: The policemen begin firing upon entering the camp.
Two members of the group are killed during the attack.
Zus issues a command to maintain silence under threat.

memories.memory_text: I shouted into the trees, 'Next one is to the head!' after Kessely got hit — they needed to know we weren’t playing, that we’d kill to protect our own.
grounded_facts: Zus issued a threat: 'Next one is to the head!' after a policeman was shot in the arm.
The threat was directed at the remaining policemen during a standoff in the forest.
Zus was positioned behind cover in the trees with others during the confrontation.

memories.memory_text: After they ran, I told Tuvia his diplomacy was shit — we should’ve killed the milkman, and now we’ve got two dead and no shelter with winter coming.
grounded_facts: Zus confronted Tuvia after the skirmish, criticizing his diplomatic approach.
Zus stated, 'It means -- we should have killed the fucking milkman. Your policy of diplomacy is shit.'
Zus emphasized the consequences: two dead, no shelter, and winter approaching.

memories.memory_text: I pulled the blood-soaked package from my coat and said, 'Medicine.' That was all he needed to know.
grounded_facts: Zus produces a blood-soaked package from his coat
Zus says 'Medicine' to Tuvia

memories.memory_text: He said 'Come back to us,' and then 'You are my brother.' I answered only, 'So you remember.' I didn't look at him.
grounded_facts: Tuvia says 'Come back to us' and 'You are my brother'
Zus responds with 'So you remember' without looking at Tuvia

memories.memory_text: I stood there while Gramov sneered and said he beat Lova because he was a Jew—then laughed like it was nothing. When Panchenko made him say 'I apologize, Jew,' I felt the weight of the glass in my hand, knowing swallowing that vodka meant accepting the insult, but refusing it would mean chaos.
grounded_facts: Zus confronted Panchenko about Gramov beating Lova.
Gramov stated he beat Lova because he was a Jew, and others laughed.
Panchenko told Gramov to apologize, and Gramov said, 'I apologize, Jew.'
Panchenko then ordered Zus to drink vodka, and after hesitation, Zus accepted and drank.

memories.scene_title: 141 LATER - A SPOTTER PLANE BUZZES HIGH ABOVE - HUNTING 141
memories.memory_text: I stood there with my rifle in hand, knowing what Viktor would say before he even said it — that my brothers were just another sacrifice for the cause, and that my loyalty to them meant nothing to him.
grounded_facts: Zus overhears Viktor giving an order via field radio as a spotter plane flies overhead.
Zus confronts Viktor, accusing him of wanting the Germans to find Tuvia's camp.
Viktor justifies potential loss as necessary for the revolutionary struggle.
Zus emphasizes 'Those are my brothers,' to which Viktor responds that his loyalty should be to the revolution, not family.
Zus grips his rifle but does not speak further after Viktor calls his loyalty 'counter-revolutionary.'
